blockingIssues.description: `gen_new_expr` only inserts explicit Go type conversions for raw numeric literal AST nodes, so negative numeric payloads parsed as unary expressions (for example `Some(-1)` / `Some(-1.5)`) still bypass the typed-literal path and leave the pointer-backed Option literal fix incomplete.
Wildcard let bindings still bypass the new nested-`?` hoisting path, so constructs like `let _ = add_ten(inner(x)?);` emit invalid Go instead of hoisted early-return checks.

The shipped `examples/result_option.rg` example is still rewritten with `show_*` wrappers and a wrapper-driven `main`, so the regression does not validate the original authored example end-to-end from the test harness as required.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker discovered a still-open typechecker limitation while building the regression test: generic associated-function calls from outside impl scope lose concrete type arguments because concrete_type_for_name falls back to TStruct(name, []) / TEnum(name, []). This constraint materially shaped the final test design, but it is not reflected in shared library docs.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented by the dedicated fix feature phase-7-fix-generic-constructor-type-argument-inference in this same milestone, so adding a separate shared-state update would be stale.
